sources.notes: Read the complete assignment/query rules, last-answer decoding, forced-online dependency, and constraints n <= 1e5, q <= 3e5, and 1 <= a_i <= n.
Read the full official tutorial; confirmed complementing unequal pairs to equal pairs, full-block interval maintenance, the row/column deferred-update split, and fringe reconstruction.

Actually read the complete Gold14526 writeup; independently cross-checked the O((n+q)*sqrt(n)) decomposition through squared prefix-frequency norms and dynamically maintained prefix-vector dot products.

Re-derived the insertion/deletion delta for every full-block interval, verified that middle-middle, middle-fringe, and fringe-fringe equal pairs are counted exactly once, and checked the need for 64-bit answers.

annotation.summary: Maintain an array under point assignments and answer encoded online range queries asking for the number of index pairs with unequal values.
annotation.constraints: n <= 1e5, q <= 3e5, and all values lie in [1,n]. Queries are decoded using the previous type-2 answer, so they cannot be reordered. A range can contain C(1e5,2) pairs, requiring 64-bit arithmetic. The 1024 MB limit permits block-prefix frequency storage of size O(n*sqrt(n)).
annotation.core_idea: For a range of length len, compute equal pairs E=sum_x C(freq_x,2) and return C(len,2)-E. Split the array into blocks of size B, with K=O(n/B) blocks. Maintain E[L][R] for every interval of complete blocks and per-value prefix counts over blocks. Inserting value x into block t changes every E[i][j] with i<=t<=j by count_x(i,j). Split this as count_x(i,t)+count_x(t+1,j): the first term is independent of j and becomes a row-suffix deferred update, while the second is independent of i and becomes a column-prefix deferred update. Difference-style update_right/update_left tables reduce both modification and retrieval of a full-block interval to O(K). For an arbitrary query, obtain the largest complete-block middle and scan the O(B) boundary elements, adding each element's pairs with the middle from block-prefix counts and its pairs with earlier fringe elements from a temporary frequency bucket.
annotation.complexity: O(n*K + q*(B+K)) time and O(n*K + K^2 + n) memory; choosing B=Theta(sqrt(n)) and K=Theta(sqrt(n)) gives O((n+q)*sqrt(n)) time and O(n*sqrt(n)) memory.
tricks: Count all pairs minus equal-value pairs instead of maintaining unequal pairs directly
Split an update contribution count_x(i,j) at the modified block so each half depends on only one interval boundary
Represent row-suffix and column-prefix additions with deferred difference-style tables
Partition query pairs into full-middle, middle-fringe, and fringe-fringe contributions
Process every encoded query immediately and retain last in 64-bit arithmetic

solution_variants.name: online-block-equal-pair-maintenance
solution_variants.summary: Use square-root decomposition, maintain equal-pair counts for all complete-block intervals through row/column deferred contribution tables, and reconstruct arbitrary ranges by scanning their two boundary fragments.

tags.evidence: The array is divided into Theta(sqrt(n)) blocks; complete-block intervals are maintained globally while only O(sqrt(n)) boundary elements are scanned per query.
The O(K^2) rectangle of affected full-block intervals is represented as row-suffix and column-prefix deferred additions, which are accumulated when a particular interval is queried.

Inserting or deleting one occurrence changes an interval's equal-pair count by exactly the number of other equal occurrences in that interval; splitting and maintaining this delta is the central update invariant.
Every operation is decoded with the previous query answer, so queries must be processed in input order and offline Mo-style reordering is unavailable.
Per-value prefix counts over complete blocks provide count_x(L,R) in O(1), supporting both point-update deltas and middle-fringe query contributions.
